title: Today's Tech Brief: That an app 'Fits on a Floppy' is still a useful measure in 2026
summary: A look at the relevance of floppy disk space in modern apps, and other tech news.

body.text: The article highlights how the push for smaller software has implications for cloud economics and security. With less data being transferred and stored, there's less risk of data breaches and lower costs for infrastructure. It's a refreshing perspective in a world where bloatware and feature creep seem to dominate the software landscape.

I've also been following news about the growing interest in low-tech solutions, like vinyl records and film cameras. It seems people are craving a more tactile experience in a world that's increasingly digital. And then there's the intersection of software strategy and customer expectations - companies are realizing that sometimes, less really is more.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.

sources.title: That an app 'Fits on a Floppy' is still a useful measure in 2026
